revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小被神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 埼玉県大里郡寄居町富田1508 | 緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 06 |緯度秒 = 37.2 | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 14 |経度秒 = 24.8 |祭神 = [[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]] |神体 = |社格 = 式内社
旧村社 |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の御代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[富田村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は郷社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の御代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる。社頭掲示板 [[寛文]]9年（1669年）には、富田村と天正8年（1580年）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村の村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した。『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より 明治時代入ると、神仏分離令により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、当社の神職となる。『新編武蔵風土記稿か』より == 社格 == === 神階 === *元治元年(1864年)3月9日 - 正一位 === 近代社格制度 === *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊社頭掲示板 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *牛頭 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日）社頭掲示板 *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日） == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩分15 = 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かわすくねしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Obusuma Shrine'''|小被神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Obusuma Shrine'''|小被神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Obusuma Shrine'''|小被神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Obusuma Shrine'''|小被神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Expand Japanese|小被神社}}

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Obusuma Shrine'''|小被神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小被神社 |画像 = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]]富田1508 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[男衾村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 [[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）には、富田村民と、[[天正]]8年（[[1580年]]）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 [[明治|明治時代]]に入ると、[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より == 境内 == File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:おふすましんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小被神社]] to [[Obusuma Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小被神社 |画像 = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]]富田1508 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[男衾村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 [[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）には、富田村民と、[[天正]]8年（[[1580年]]）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 [[明治|明治時代]]に入ると、[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より == 境内 == File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:おふすましんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[男衾村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 [[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）には、富田村民と、[[天正]]8年（[[1580年]]）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 [[明治|明治時代]]に入ると、[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より == 境内 == File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[男衾村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 [[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）には、富田村民と、[[天正]]8年（[[1580年]]）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 [[明治|明治時代]]に入ると、[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より == 境内 == File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （社伝）[[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]][[男衾村|富田]]の[[荒川 (関東)|荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]。[[武蔵国]][[男衾郡]]の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 == 由緒 == [[安閑天皇]]の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 [[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）には、富田村民と、[[天正]]8年（[[1580年]]）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 [[明治|明治時代]]に入ると、[[神仏分離|神仏分離令]]により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *[[元治]]元年（[[1864年]]）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 == 祭神 == *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より == 境内 == File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 == 境内社 == === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 == 祭事 == *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より == 交通 == *[[東武東上線]][[男衾駅]]から徒歩15分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 小被神社 |画像 = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|大里郡|Q1075196}}{{jalink|寄居町|Q961604}}富田1508 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|瓊瓊杵尊}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = （社伝）{{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}の代（531年～535年） |宮司 = |本殿 = 見世棚造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 4月14日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=小被神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''小被神社'''（おぶすまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|大里郡|Q1075196}}{{jalink|寄居町|Q961604}}{{jalink|男衾村|Q11578176|富田}}の{{jalink|荒川 (関東)|Q625465|荒川}}沿いにある神社。祭神は{{jalink|ニニギ|Q1056017|瓊瓊杵尊}}。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|男衾郡|Q11578175}}の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|安閑天皇|Q241765}}の代（531年～535年）に、富田鹿が富田村字塚越（現在の大聖山真言院不動寺境内）に祠を建て、小被神を祀ったと伝えられる社頭掲示板より。 {{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}9年（{{jalink|1669年|Q7602}}）には、富田村民と、{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}8年（{{jalink|1580年|Q6712}}）の荒川の大洪水で移ってきた赤浜村民の間で発生した領境論争により、現在の鎮座地に移転した『武州男衾郡上下富田村与赤浜村境論之事』より。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096|明治時代}}に入ると、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535|神仏分離令}}により別当寺「不動寺」の僧が還俗し、同社の神職となった『新編武蔵風土記稿』より。 === 社格 === ==== 神階 ==== *{{jalink|元治|Q1325419}}元年（{{jalink|1864年|Q7704}}）3月9日 - 正一位『宗源宣旨』より ==== 近代社格制度 ==== *明治4年11月11日 - 村社 *明治41年7月1日 - 神饌幣帛料供進社 === 祭神 === *（主神）瓊瓊杵尊 *（配祀）木花咲耶姫命、彦火火出見尊社頭掲示板より === 境内 === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|一の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|二の鳥居 File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|拝殿 File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|神楽殿 === 境内社 === === 摂社 === *伊勢原 神明神社 *大久保 愛宕神社 *鳥羽 白山神社 *塚越 稲荷神社 *下郷 内の宮社 *頭牛 天王宮 === 末社 === *鷲丸 冨士浅間大神 *堂ノ入 山神社 *谷戸 山神社 === 祭事 === *新年祭（1月1日） *祈年祭 （4月第2日曜日） *新嘗祭 （10月第2日曜日） *大祓 （12月31日）社頭掲示板より === 交通 === *{{jalink|東武東上線|Q11527536}}{{jalink|男衾駅|Q7075604}}から徒歩15分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:おふすましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:寄居町の建築物]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Obusuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}{{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465}} in {{jalink|Tomita|Q11578176|Obusuma Village}}, {{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}}, {{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Obusuma District|Q11578175}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1669|Q7602}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}}, due to the {{jalink|Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{jalink|Genji|Q1325419}} ({{jalink|1864|Q7704}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Obusuma Station|Q7075604}} on the {{jalink|Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Obusuma Station|Q7075604}} on the {{jalink|Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Obusuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}{{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465}} in {{jalink|Tomita|Q11578176|Obusuma Village}}, {{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}}, {{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Obusuma District|Q11578175}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1669|Q7602}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}}, due to the {{jalink|Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{jalink|Genji|Q1325419}} ({{jalink|1864|Q7704}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Obusuma Station|Q7075604}} on the {{jalink|Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Obusuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}{{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465}} in {{jalink|Tomita|Q11578176|Obusuma Village}}, {{jalink|Yorii Town|Q961604}}, {{jalink|Ōsato District|Q1075196}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}{{jalink|Obusuma District|Q11578175}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Ankan|Q241765}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1669|Q7602}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} ({{jalink|1580|Q6712}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{jalink|Meiji era|Q3125096}}, due to the {{jalink|Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{jalink|Genji|Q1325419}} ({{jalink|1864|Q7704}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Obusuma Station|Q7075604}} on the {{jalink|Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obusumajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196}}{{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obusumajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obusumajinja}} == {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village|1=Obusuma}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175|1=Obusuma district}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602|1=1669}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712|1=1580}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604|1=Obusuma Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obusumajinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Obusuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village|1=Obusuma}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175|1=Obusuma district}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602|1=1669}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712|1=1580}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604|1=Obusuma Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma category|Category:Obusuma-jinja}} [[JA:小被神社]] {{wikidata link|Q66105394}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Obusuma Shrine |Image = [[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village|1=Obusuma}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175|1=Obusuma district}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602|1=1669}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712|1=1580}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604|1=Obusuma Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village|1=Obusuma}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175|1=Obusuma district}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602|1=1669}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712|1=1580}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604|1=Obusuma Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Yorii Town]] [[Category:寄居町の歴史]]

[[File:Obusuma-jinja.JPG|270px]] |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}{{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}} Tomita 1508 |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Shrine tradition) Reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535) |Chief priest = |Main hall = Mise-dana-zukuri |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = April 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=6|lat_sec=37.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=14|lon_sec=24.8|label=Obusuma Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|6|37.2|N|139|14|24.8|E}}}} }} '''Obusuma Shrine''' (おぶすまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Arakawa River (Kantō)|Q625465|WD=Q625465|1=Arakawa River (Kantō)}} in {{ill|lt=Tomita|Q11578176|WD=Q11578176|lt=Obusuma Village|1=Obusuma}}, {{ill|lt=Yorii Town|Q961604|WD=Q961604|1=Yorii, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Ōsato District|Q1075196|WD=Q1075196|1=Ōsato District, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}. It is a Shikinaisha (listed shrine) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}{{ill|lt=Obusuma District|Q11578175|WD=Q11578175|1=Obusuma district}}. Its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History === According to tradition, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}} (531–535), Tomita Shika built a small shrine in Tsukagoshi, Tomita Village (present-day grounds of Daishōzan Shingon-in Fudō-ji Temple) and enshrined the Obusuma deity there.From the shrine notice board In the 9th year of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|WD=Q7602|1=1669}}), due to a territorial boundary dispute between villagers of Tomita and villagers of Akahama—who had relocated there after the great flood of the Arakawa River in the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|WD=Q6712|1=1580}})—the shrine was moved to its current location.From “Bushū Obusuma-gun Kamishimo Tomita-mura to Akahama-mura Boundary Dispute” With the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism edict|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of the affiliated temple “Fudō-ji” returned to lay life and became a Shinto priest of the shrine.From “Newly Compiled Musashi Fudoki” === Shrine status === ==== Divine rank ==== * 9 March, 1st year of {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) – Senior First RankFrom “Sōgen Senji” ==== Modern shrine ranking system ==== * November 11, Meiji 4 – Village shrine * July 1, Meiji 41 – Shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and paper streamers === Enshrined deities === * (Main deity) Ninigi-no-Mikoto * (Associated deities) Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto, Hikohohodemi-no-MikotoFrom the shrine notice board === Shrine grounds === File:Obusuma-jinja Torii1.JPG|First torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Torii2.JPG|Second torii gate File:Obusuma-jinja Worship Hall.JPG|Worship hall File:Obusuma-jinja Kagura Hall.JPG|Kagura hall === Auxiliary shrines === === Subsidiary shrines === * Isehara Shinmei Shrine * Ōkubo Atago Shrine * Toba Hakusan Shrine * Tsukagoshi Inari Shrine * Shimogō Uchi-no-Miya Shrine * Tōgyū Ten’ō Shrine === Minor shrines === * Washimaru Fuji Asama Ōkami * Dōnoiri Yama Shrine * Yato Yama Shrine === Festivals === * New Year Festival (January 1) * Kinen-sai (second Sunday of April) * Niiname-sai (second Sunday of October) * Ōharae (December 31)From the shrine notice board === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obusuma Station|Q7075604|WD=Q7075604|1=Obusuma Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōbu Tōjō Line|Q11527536|WD=Q11527536|1=Tōbu Tōjō Lines}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Obusuma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama
